mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE remains below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings revision channels.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60d returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5d SPX returns during periods of high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.0%), or the signal reverses direction in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 70% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is already above 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days via wage-growth and consumption channels.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60d returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20d SPX returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.8% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain disruptions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO drops > 0.8%, or the signal fails to predict returns when the decline is driven by seasonal adjustments.
